frameworks.name: intrinsic neural timescales
frameworks.one_line: hierarchy of temporal windows over which spontaneous brain activity integrates

frameworks.name: autocorrelation / power-law spectral analysis
frameworks.one_line: ACW, PSD and PLE quantify temporal structure of resting EEG

frameworks.name: sensory vs motor information processing dichotomy
frameworks.one_line: abnormal behavioral states dissociate sensory from motor deficits

core_question: Are intrinsic neural timescales of spontaneous brain activity tied to sensory rather than motor information processing?
key_assumptions: ACW, PSD and PLE jointly index intrinsic timescale
abnormal behavioral states can dissociate sensory vs motor capacity
resting-state EEG reflects intrinsic temporal architecture
manipulations: patient group: motor deficits (ALS, locked-in) vs sensory deficits (anesthesia, UWS)
progressive sensory reduction across sleep stages (awake, N1, N2, N3)
key_findings: prolonged intrinsic timescales in conditions with sensory deficits
shift toward slower frequencies in sensory-deficit conditions
no comparable timescale prolongation in motor-deficit conditions
interpretations: intrinsic neural timescale specifically supports sensory rather than motor information processing
